protocol.name: ext_idle_notify_v1

protocol.description:
This protocol allows clients to be notified when the user is idle for a given amount of time.
A compositor may not support idle notifications at all, in which case the global will not be advertised.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the staging phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: ext_idle_notifier_v1
interface.description: This interface allows a client to get notified when the user is idle.

request.name: get_idle_notification
request.description:
This creates an idle notification with the given timeout. After the client has been idle for the specified amount of time, the compositor will send the idled event to the notification object. When the user becomes active again, the resumed event is sent.
The notification object remains valid until the client destroys it, or the compositor cancels the notification (e.g., screen locks). The compositor may resume notifications after screen unlock.
The timeout is given in milliseconds with a minimum of 100 ms.

interface.name: ext_idle_notification_v1
interface.description: An idle notification object created by the idle notifier.

event.name: idled
event.description: This event is sent when the user has been idle for the time specified when creating the notification object.

event.name: resumed
event.description: This event is sent when the user is no longer idle.
